summary: Backend-only verification of the new POST /api/ai/first-message-from-bio endpoint plus regression smoke for /api/ai/first-message and /api/ai/screenshot. All 9 new tests pass and the entire historical backend suite (35 existing tests across Round 1–3) still passes — total 44/44 green in 86s. Happy-path returns a non-empty list of string suggestions (4 requested, AI returned 4) for a Pillow-generated fake bio JPEG with extra_context='We matched on Hinge'. Missing Authorization header → 401, invalid bearer token → 401, empty image_base64 → 400, and a `data:image/jpeg;base64,` prefix is correctly stripped server-side. Response payload contains no Mongo ObjectId leak. Regression: /api/ai/first-message (text) and /api/ai/screenshot continue to return 200 with valid suggestions arrays. No backend code changes were required.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:786-833 — /api/ai/first-message-from-bio implementation mirrors /api/ai/screenshot exactly: auth → mode-gating → prompt build → data-URL prefix strip → 400 on empty b64 → Claude call → JSON-list parse → suffix-trim to req.count → safety check → 422 SAFETY_BLOCKED with descriptive payload. Consistent ordering, no async/await issues, no _id leakage, env-driven keys via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY.
BioOpenerRequest model (server.py:69-74) is identical in shape to ScreenshotRequest — DRY candidate but acceptable since semantics differ.
Both endpoints rely on the same call_claude/parse_json_list/check_safety_block primitives — central, well-tested code path.
server.py is now ~1301 lines (was 994 last iteration). Approaching the point where splitting into modules (auth, ai, payments, translate, history) becomes worthwhile — non-blocking for this review.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_bio_opener.py (new — 9 tests covering bio-opener happy path, data-URL prefix stripping, auth gating, bad-token, empty-b64 400, ObjectId-leak check, and regression smoke for /api/ai/first-message + /api/ai/screenshot)
seed_data_creation: Module-scoped dev-login user TEST_bio_<uuid8>@loveassist.ai created via POST /api/auth/dev-login for the bio-opener tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New endpoint POST /api/ai/first-message-from-bio is live and behaves identically to /api/ai/screenshot in terms of auth/validation/safety semantics — only the system prompt and user-text differ (asks for opening DMs instead of replies). Tests in /app/backend/tests/test_bio_opener.py are self-contained (module-scoped fixture) and runnable alongside the rest of the suite. AI calls take ~5–10s each so suite runs in ~30s when run alone, ~86s for the full backend suite.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Feature works end-to-end as specified.
